topic: A person finds their spouse's secret savings account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the apartment was thick, amplifying the frantic drumming in my chest. I was sorting through old tax documents, a mundane task that suddenly felt monumental. Tucked inside a brittle envelope, I found it—a small, unassuming bank statement with an unfamiliar account number. My hands started to tremble, the paper crinkling under my touch like dry leaves. A cold dread washed over me, a sudden, sharp awareness that the foundation of my shared life was built on something I hadn't seen. I looked toward the bedroom door, half-expecting him to walk in, ready to explain, ready to face the sudden, gaping chasm opening beneath my feet.

[story 2]

I kept catching my reflection in the darkened windowpane, my breath catching in shallow gasps. The discovery felt like a physical blow, stealing the air from my lungs. It wasn't the money itself that caused the immediate panic; it was the implication—the hidden life, the separate reality I hadn't been privy to. I retreated to the kitchen, gripping the edge of the counter until my knuckles were white. Every shadow seemed to shift, and the low hum of the refrigerator sounded like a warning siren. I spent a long minute just staring at my own trembling fingers, trying to steady the internal storm before I could speak, before the reality of what I held dissolved me entirely.

[story 3]

He was out late, leaving a faint scent of sandalwood lingering in the air, and I was alone with the evidence. The bank statement lay on the mahogany desk, stark white against the dark wood. A wave of nausea rolled over me, making my vision swim slightly. I picked up the paper, my movements slow and deliberate, as if handling something fragile and dangerous. My throat tightened, making it hard to draw a full breath. I could feel a profound sense of exposure, as if every carefully constructed layer of trust I possessed had just been peeled away, revealing a hollow space beneath. I finally dropped the paper, the sound barely audible, and sank into the nearest armchair, letting the quiet engulf me.
